meta.title: About · physics
meta.description: About Physics.explained — a visual-first reader's guide to the classical and modern worlds.

header.title: WHO WE ARE
header.subtitle: A small studio building a visual, honest reader's guide to physics.

idea.title: The idea
idea.p1: Most physics writing either drowns you in equations or waves its hands until the math disappears. Neither works. We wanted a third option — explanations where the pictures do the heavy lifting, the equations show up only where they earn their keep, and every animation is physically accurate.
idea.p2: Every scene on this site is a real simulation. Not a video, not a loop — the pendulums actually swing under θ″ = −(g/L)·sin θ, the orbits actually obey Kepler's laws. If you can see it, you can trust it.

studio.title: The studio
studio.p1: Physics.explained is made by Simnetiq Ltd, a small independent studio working on tools and writing at the edge of science, design, and code.
studio.p2: The project is open source under MIT. No tracking, no ads, no paywall. If you want to fund more branches, corrections, or translations — say hi below.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Spotted an error? Have a branch you'd like to see next? Want to collaborate? Drop us a line.
